message:
test(serve): gate branch tests on full Search, not SearchQuick, with 10s budget

cassSearchOperational is the per-test gate that decides whether the
branch-reachability integration tests run their full
cass-depending assertions or skip. The previous implementation
called client.SearchQuick with a 5s timeout, which was hitting
two real failure modes on the VPS:

1. SearchQuick follows a different code path than the handler's
   real full-Search call (the one the tests actually exercise via
   their HTTP fixtures). A healthy SearchQuick probe therefore
   didn't prove the thing the tests depend on: that a full Search
   can complete inside the handler's own 30s budget. Tests were
   running against a backend that immediately timed out under the
   handler's own ctx, producing flaky red results that weren't
   caught by the gate.

2. The 5s probe budget was too tight for a VPS running multiple
   concurrent agent workloads — cass could be fully operational
   but slow enough under peer load to blow 5s even on SearchQuick,
   making the gate too pessimistic and skipping tests that would
   have passed if they'd had the handler's real budget.

Switch the probe to the real client.Search entry point with
SearchOptions{Query: "test", Limit: 1}, and raise the probe
timeout to 10s — still well below the handler's own 30s budget,
so a green gate meaningfully implies "full Search will complete
inside the handler timeout". Limit: 1 keeps the probe
lightweight on the server side. Add a comment above the ctx
construction explaining the budget choice so a future agent
doesn't walk the number back down without understanding why.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5685,10 +5685,14 @@ func cassSearchOperational(t *testing.T) bool {
 		return false
 	}
 
-	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
+	// Exercise the same code path (Search, not SearchQuick) with a budget
+	// well below the handler's 30s timeout. Under VPS load cass can be slow
+	// enough that the handler-side ctx gets killed even when SearchQuick was
+	// fast, so gate on a realistic full Search completing promptly.
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
 	defer cancel()
 
-	_, err := client.SearchQuick(ctx, "test")
+	_, err := client.Search(ctx, cass.SearchOptions{Query: "test", Limit: 1})
 	return err == nil
 }
